today_summary: GCM26 spot opens at $4,096 with ATM IV at 23.67% and a 1σ daily range of ±$61, bracketing today’s session to $4,035–$4,157 absent a macro catalyst. PC-OI ratio of 0.59 confirms continued call-side dominance; total call OI of 127,378 vs 75,678 puts reflects structural bullish positioning despite a $106 slide from the 5-day high. Net GEX in the ±25% window is −129 — dealers remain short gamma and will amplify moves in both directions, making reversals sharper than IV alone implies. Max pain at $4,800 sits $704 above spot with 30 DTE remaining on GCM26.
five_day_thesis: Over the past five sessions spot shed $106.5 (4,202 → 4,096) while max pain compressed only $35 (4,835 → 4,800), widening the spot-to-max-pain gap to $704 — the widest stretch in the window and a structural argument for eventual mean-reversion toward the expiry magnet. The defining test was June 25–26 when spot touched $4,009 and IV spiked to 26.43%, forcing a near-breach of the $4,000 put wall (4,326 contracts); the wall held, spot bounced $86 over three sessions, and IV partially compressed to 23.67% — still +1.61pp vs five days ago, confirming the options market carries a residual fear premium into today. The $4,000 put wall has been absolutely immovable for the entire 5-session window with zero migration in put_wall_migration, meaning dealers have committed meaningful negative-gamma short-covering support at precisely this level. Net GEX improved from the weekly extreme of −241 (June 25, the put-wall test) to −129 today, signaling dealer short-gamma exposure diminished as spot recovered — but the negative posture persists, and a breakdown below $4,000 would re-trigger amplification. The $5,370 call wall (8,010 OI) has been equally frozen throughout the week, defining an untested ceiling $1,274 above current price; near-term actionable resistance shifts to $4,400–$4,700 where call OI concentrations of 3,950–4,591 contracts sit within the 1σ–2σ range.
what_changed: Spot −$106.5 over 5 sessions (4,202 → 4,096); spot tested the $4,000 put wall on June 25 (low: $4,009) and bounced $86 into today’s open.
Max pain −$35 to $4,800; expiry magnet holds far above spot, widening the gap to $704 — the widest reading in the 5-day window.
ATM IV +1.61pp net to 23.67%; intraweek spike to 26.43% on June 26 during the put-wall test confirms elevated realized vol and residual risk premium.
Net GEX improved from −241 (June 25) to −129 today; dealers remain short gamma but the acute amplification regime softened as spot rebounded from $4,000.
GEX flip strike migrated 3,365 → 3,225 (June 25 low) → 3,280 today — bottomed at the put-wall test and is now edging back, but remains 816 points below spot.
Call OI +11,036 (+9.5%) vs put OI +4,125 (+5.8%) over 5 sessions; call accumulation outpacing puts, PC-OI dipped to 0.59, reflecting institutional call buying above $4,500.
prediction: Spot is likely to consolidate the $4,000–$4,150 corridor with a mild upward lean as the immovable $4,000 put wall pins the downside and positive GEX at $4,125–$4,300 creates a dealer buy-the-dip dynamic above the flip zone. A break and close above $4,165 (positive GEX cluster, net_gex +2.89) opens $4,200–$4,250 with limited resistance; above $4,300 the net GEX swings strongly positive (+25.98), capping directional moves and inviting range-fade strategies. Downside risk: a confident break of $4,000 on volume removes the put wall buffer and triggers negative-GEX amplification toward $3,900 (put OI 3,040). With 30 DTE and max pain $704 above, expiry pull is minimal today — price action will be driven by the dealer floor at $4,000 versus any intraday macro catalysts.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Net GEX −129 means dealers are short gamma and moves amplify in both directions. Long bias reflects the immovable $4,000 put wall and the 3-session bounce already in progress; fade zone targets the positive-GEX ceiling at $4,125–$4,200 where upside momentum degrades.
day_trade_cta.entry_zone_note: ATM cluster zone; spot is consolidating near these levels after the $4,000 bounce. Entries here receive put-wall support 60–95 points below and capture the gap to the $4,125 positive-GEX flip.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: $4,000 is the 5-session put OI anchor (4,326 contracts, zero migration all week). An intraday close below $4,000 with volume confirms wall failure and switches bias to short targeting $3,900.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: 25-point clearance below the $4,000 put wall. A print below $3,975 confirms structural failure; net_gex at $3,975 is −2.35 (short gamma), so losses can accelerate quickly toward $3,900.
day_trade_cta.targets_note: $4,150 = 1σ upside from open ($61 move) and a put OI concentration (2,637 contracts) that acts as a near-term magnet. $4,200 = prior week’s consolidation zone; net_gex near zero (+0.39) signals diminishing dealer support for further upside.
